title: Identification and Validation of Prognostic Genes Associated with m6A-Regulated Programmed Cell Death in Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1r2v7","text":"This study investigated the prognostic significance of m6A-related programmed cell death (PCD) genes in acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL). Transcriptomic data from the TCGA-ALL and GSE48558 datasets were analyzed to identify m6A-related PCD genes (corgenes), which were then intersected with 3,063 differentially expressed\ngenes (DEGs), resulting in 23 candidate genes. Univariate Cox regression and LASSO regression analyses identified CFLAR and CDK4 as key prognostic genes, facilitating the construction of a high-accuracy risk prediction model. Gene set enrichment analysis (GSEA) revealed significant pathway differences between the high-risk group (HRG) and the low-risk group (LRG), including olfactory transduction, circadian rhythm, and protein export (adj.P \u003c 0.05). Sensitivity analysis of 60 chemotherapy agents indicated that Bicalutamide was more effective in LRG, while ATRA, CCT018159,PHA-665752, and PLX4720 demonstrated greater efficacy in HRG. RT-qPCR validation confirmed upregulation of CDK4 and downregulation of CFLAR in ALL samples (P \u003c 0.05). This study offers important theoretical support for the prognostic evaluation andpersonalized treatment strategies in ALL.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":767,"length":1},{"style":"italic","offset":1058,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7eqcn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Data Source and Preprocessing Purpose: Acquire transcriptome data and gene lists for bioinformatics analysis. Outcome %s %s
Acquisition of Candidate Genes： Identify m6A-regulated PCD genes via correlation and differential expression analyses. %s %s
Functional Enrichment and PPI Network: Annotate biological functions and protein interactions of candidate genes. %s %s
Identification of Prognostic Genes： Screen OS-related genes via survival analyses. %s %s
RT-qPCR Validation： Verify CFLAR/CDK4 expression in clinical samples.
Prognostic Model Construction and Evaluation: Develop and validate a risk model based on CFLAR and CDK4. %s %s
Software: R v4.3.1 (ggplot2, survival), GraphPad Prism 10. Tests: t-test/Wilcoxon rank-sum test for group comparisons; log-rank test for survival. Significance: P < 0.05.
creator: Min He
